title: Galeopsis cebennensis
intro: Galeopsis cebennensis, also known as Cebennian Galeopsis, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe and calm the body. The bioactive constituents of Galeopsis cebennensis include iridoid glycosides, such as cebennoside, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Galeopsis cebennensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Galeopsis cebennensis is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Lamiaceae. The species is further classified within the genus Galeopsis, which includes several species of hemp-nettle and hemp-nettle relatives. The plant's classification at the species level is within the family Lamiaceae, which includes plants such as mint, basil, and oregano. This classification helps to identify the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
